paper_title: When are off-diagonal hypergraph Ramsey numbers polynomial?
summary: Conjecture 4.2 from arXiv:2411.13812 posits a tower-height lower bound $t_s$ for the Ramsey number $r(H, K_n^{(k)})$ whenever $H$ is $s$-tightly connected, $k > s$, and $H$ is not $k$-partite, generalising the $k=3$, $s=2$ case proved as Theorem 4.1 in the same paper. The authors themselves describe the conjecture as 'likely difficult'. A broad web search spanning arXiv and journal sources up to May 2026 found no follow-up paper proving, disproving, or making substantial partial progress on this conjecture. Several related recent papers (e.g., arXiv:2603.16069 on quasipolynomial hypergraph Ramsey growth rates, arXiv:2502.20863 on bounded-degree hypergraph Ramsey lower bounds) work in adjacent territory but do not address this specific statement.
search_queries: hypergraph Ramsey numbers s-tightly connected tower function lower bound 2411.13812
off-diagonal hypergraph Ramsey polynomial Conlon Fox Mubayi Suk tightly connected k-partite
arXiv 2025 2026 s-tightly connected k-graph hypergraph Ramsey tower height conjecture Conlon Fox
notes: No follow-up found. The conjecture is very recent (paper first posted November 2024, revised October 2025) and is explicitly flagged as 'likely difficult' by the authors. Adjacent work on quasipolynomial growth rates (arXiv:2603.16069) and bounded-degree lower bounds (arXiv:2502.20863) does not address this conjecture.
